commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `approvals.plugin.language` config with plain-language and simple-technical plugin approval rendering, wires it through shared forwarding plus Telegram/Matrix native approval payloads, and updates schema, docs, and tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking review finding: source inspection gives a concrete PR-head path where `Command: ssh -o ProxyCommand='sh -c "rm -rf /tmp/x"' host` is classified as an SSH connection instead of local shell execution. I did not run the renderer because this review was read-only, but the code path and OpenSSH contract are clear.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can make SSH local-execution options fail closed and add focused renderer tests, though final merge still needs human approval-safety review.

target.title: feat(approvals): add plain-language plugin approvals
target.body:
## Why

Plugin approval prompts are often delivered in chat, but the previous fallback copy read like internal debug output. A normal approval showed raw title, command text, proposed exec policy, session key, tool/plugin IDs, expiry, and `/approve <id>` syntax. That is useful for debugging, but it is hard to scan in Telegram/Slack-style flows and makes safe approvals feel more confusing than they are.

This PR adds a configurable plain-language approval path while keeping the original raw format available.

## What Changed

- Added `approvals.plugin.language`:
  - `original`: keep the raw legacy approval text.
  - `simple`: show only the plain-English approval prompt.
  - `simple-technical`: show the plain-English prompt plus raw technical fields below it.
- Left default behavior unchanged by keeping the default as `original`.
- Reworked `simple` mode into a scan-friendly card shape: `Action`, `It will`, `Risk`, and `Choose below`.
- Removed raw technical details from `simple`: no title/description dump, session, tool, plugin, ID, expiry, proposed exec policy, or `/approve <id>` syntax.
- Added a bounded `Command preview` for incomplete command summaries, with token/password-looking values redacted.
- Kept interactive approval metadata intact so Telegram/Slack-style UIs can still render Allow Once / Allow Always / Deny buttons.
- Wired the language option through the shared plugin approval renderer and fallback approval forwarder.
- Updated config schema/help/tests for the new setting.

Example config:

```json
{
  "approvals": {
    "plugin": {
      "language": "simple"
    }
  }
}
```

## Command Translation Surface

The simple renderer now covers the approval shapes that showed up in real Codex app-server usage:

- wrappers/plumbing: `timeout`, `bash -lc`, `sh -c`, `zsh -c`, `set -a`, `source`, `[ -f ... ]`, `test`;
- escaped chat payloads: decodes `&&`, `<`, `>`, quotes, and numeric HTML entities before shell summarization;
- shell control flow: summarizes `if` / `elif` / `while` / `until` conditions, unwraps `then` / `else` bodies, and hides `fi` / `done` bookkeeping;
- low-signal helpers: `sleep`, `printf`, `echo`, formatting pipes;
- process/log checks: `ps`, `pgrep`, `tail`, `head`, `cat`, `sed`, `journalctl`;
- file inspection: `pwd`, `ls`, `find`, `rg`, `grep`, `wc`, `stat`, `file`, `du`, `df`;
- file changes: `mkdir`, `touch`, `cp`, `mv`, `rm`, `chmod`, `chown`;
- network/API calls: `curl`, `wget` with auth-looking arguments redacted from the simple prompt;
- dev/ops tooling: `npm`, `pnpm`, `yarn`, `bun`, `node`, `python`, `npx`, `git`, `docker`, `systemctl`, `ss`, `lsof`.

Risk handling fails closed for shell expansions like `$()`, backticks, and process substitution instead of translating them as harmless text. Ordinary pipelines are expanded stage-by-stage; unknown pipeline stages are marked high-risk before simple mode hides the raw technical command.

When the renderer still cannot fully summarize a command, `simple` mode now includes a short `Command preview` so the user can see the basic command shape without reintroducing the full technical dump. The preview is capped and redacts common bearer-token, password, API key, token flag, and URL credential forms.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/plugin-approval-forwarder.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.runtime-config.config.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm lint:core`

## Live Smoke

Cherry-picked onto the Hadi gateway source checkout for live testing, ran `pnpm build`, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, and verified:

- live config is `approvals.plugin.language: simple`;
- gateway is active and loopback-bound on `127.0.0.1:18789`;
- Telegram default account is enabled, running, connected, and polling;
- live renderer output includes the manual `/approve <id> ...` fallback in `simple` mode for text-only channels.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Chat-delivered plugin approvals in `simple` mode now render as a short plain-English approval card instead of a raw technical dump. Common Codex app-server commands are translated into readable action bullets. The screenshot failure where `&&` reached the renderer as `&&` and produced `run &amp`, `run if`, and `run then` is now covered. The security review finding for hidden non-shell pipeline stages is also covered.
- Real environment tested: Hadi's live OpenClaw source checkout on Ubuntu 24.04 (`/home/hadi/src/openclaw`) and the clean PR worktree (`/home/hadi/src/openclaw-pr-plugin-approval-language`) with Telegram channel configured in polling mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Rendered real approval messages through `buildPluginApprovalRequestMessage(..., { language: "simple" })` in the PR worktree and live gateway checkout using `pnpm exec tsx -`.
  - Ran focused approval tests, source typecheck, and core lint in the PR worktree.
  - Cherry-picked the branch onto `/home/hadi/src/openclaw`, ran `pnpm build`, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, then checked gateway and Telegram channel state.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the real renderer and live gateway checks:

```text
🛡️ Approval needed
Action
Create/check workspace files or folders

It will
- create folder(s): memory
- check whether a condition or file exists: memory/context.md

Risk: Low
Only creates or checks workspace files. No network, service, or delete step detected.

Choose below.
If buttons are unavailable, reply: /approve plugin:example allow-once|allow-always|deny

--- pipeline curl ---
🛡️ Approval needed
Action
Use the network or download data

It will
- read file contents: notes.txt
- make a network request or download data: https://example.test/upload

Risk: Medium
Network commands can send or fetch data outside this machine.

Choose below.
If buttons are unavailable, reply: /approve plugin:pipeline allow-once|allow-always|deny

--- unknown pipeline ---
🛡️ Approval needed
Action
Run a terminal command

It will
- read file contents: notes.txt
- run custom-uploader

Command preview
cat notes.txt | custom-uploader --send

Risk: High
This command is part of a pipeline I cannot fully summarize, so review it before approving.

Choose below.
If buttons are unavailable, reply: /approve plugin:unknown allow-once|allow-always|deny

- Telegram default (Telegram): enabled, configured, running, connected, mode:polling, token:tokenFile
```

- Observed result after fix: `simple` output no longer includes the technical details block, raw tool/plugin/session/ID fields, proposed exec policy, expiry, or `/approve <id>` command syntax. Escaped shell separators are decoded before parsing, so `&&` no longer becomes fake actions. Shell conditional wrappers no longer appear as `run if` / `run then`; the renderer reports the condition or inner action instead. Non-shell pipeline stages are now visible before the raw command is hidden, unknown pipeline stages are high-risk, and incomplete summaries include a redacted command preview.
- What was not tested: Native Telegram mobile screenshot proof after this latest escaped-shell/command-preview fix is still expected from Hadi's manual retest; this proof covers the actual renderer output plus live gateway/channel deployment state.
